title: Hyperspectral Spectral Unmixing
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Hyperspectral Spectral Unmixing: Hyperspectral unmixing: estimate per-pixel endmember abundances from mixed pixel spectra assuming linear mixing model. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.mixing.linear_model…); recovery is posed as a linear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~50; endmember_variability, nonlinear_mixing_fraction set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: y = M * a + n; M is endmember matrix [L x K], a is abundance vector [K x 1], sum(a) = 1, a >= 0
E.world_state_x: abundance fractions a_k(x,y) for K endmembers across H x W image
E.observation_y: hyperspectral image Y in R^{H x W x L} at L spectral bands

W.regime: Existence of the recovered abundance_map is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 50); endmember_variability dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: abundance RMSE < 0.05 for SNR > 30 dB. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~50. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |endmember_variability|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * K * L) per unmixing iteration per iteration
